summary: Tested the Survey v2 restructure (24 steps, decision/hide/auto_complete/block). All backend behaviour works correctly. Frontend user flow confirmed end-to-end for upload path. One notable React warning in UserDashboard ('Maximum update depth exceeded') floods the console but does not block the happy path.

minor.issue: FACHRICHTUNGEN list in seed_survey_v2.py currently has 35 options, review_request expected 36. Either update FACHRICHTUNGEN to include the missing Fachrichtung or adjust the spec.

ui_bugs.issue: Console repeatedly logs 'Maximum update depth exceeded. This can happen when a component calls setState inside useEffect...'. Indicates a useEffect dependency causing infinite re-renders. Non-blocking (UI still works) but will impact perf & may intermittently cause Playwright 'element not visible' races.

Duplicate data-testid='decision-option-0' rendered twice (likely mobile + desktop variants). Tests must use .first; ideally give the hidden variant a different testid or use CSS to avoid duplicate testids.

action_items: Investigate the infinite setState loop in UserDashboard (evaluateStepConditions / visibleSteps useEffect dependency list) — Maximum update depth warning.
Either deduplicate the duplicate data-testid='decision-option-N' buttons or differentiate mobile/desktop testids.
Decide whether FACHRICHTUNGEN should have 35 or 36 options (spec said 36, seed has 35).
critical_code_review_comments: admin_update_user_progress and partner_update_user_progress both correctly call apply_auto_completes after update — auto_complete therefore fires regardless of who updates progress. Confirmed.

helpers._evaluate_condition: when `field` is empty string the check `field in data` is False so it falls back to `source.get('status')` — that is why block(5) with field='' operator=status_not works. Consider making this explicit by skipping field lookup when operator is status_is/status_not.

calculate_completion_pct correctly uses countable_steps denominator that excludes hidden steps; upload-path vs partner-path users therefore have comparable but different denominators. Verified via admin /users endpoint.
seed_survey_v2 resets all user_progress and partner_submissions on every run — destructive but documented. Do not run in production without a guard.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (upload decision -> milestone auto-complete -> Fachsprachen unlocked all verified; not exercised: partner path UI, Jobangebote selbst/partner_nutzen UI, Stammdaten select options, PartnerDashboard hidden-step filtering)

seed_data_creation: Re-ran /app/backend/seed_survey_v2.py to restore deterministic seed (the previous smoke test had mutated dr.kumar so step 2 had decision=upload and step 5 was auto_completed). After reseed kumar is back to only step 1 done.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite /app/backend/tests/test_survey_v2.py is comprehensive for step structure, visibility, auto_complete & block, completion_pct. Use `pytest backend/tests/test_survey_v2.py` from /app. If the order of other tests in the module changes Kumar's starting state again, simply re-run `cd /app/backend && python3 seed_survey_v2.py` before the visibility/autocomplete tests. Frontend: I verified upload path but did NOT test partner-selection, Jobangebote multi-partner, PartnerDashboard hidden-step filtering, and full Stammdaten field rendering. Recommend targeted follow-up once the React infinite-loop warning is fixed — the loop slows the page and occasionally makes click() calls flaky.
rca of the issue: No blocker bugs. The two identified issues: (1) 'Maximum update depth exceeded' — most likely cause is that evaluateStepConditions or visibleSteps memoisation is missing or its dependency array contains an object that is recreated on every render (e.g. steps/progress arrays). Fix: wrap with useMemo keyed on stable primitives (ids+statuses) or move derivation into reducer. (2) Duplicate data-testid — UserDashboard.js around line 475 likely renders desktop+mobile variants; add index suffix or hide attr on hidden variant.
